note: Defined host/session-backed provider handoff contract: engine-created request JSON, sub-agent-created response envelope, ProviderResponse payload, identity verification, and same validation/render/ledger/archive/reconcile ingest path.

body: Claude review of 094c473 identified two-phase provider handoff blockers. Updated docs to require persisted request rebinding for phase 2, per-phase locking and duplicate checks, canonical provider=session with privacy.allow_session_provider, advisory provider-supplied person IDs, provider_host provenance, provider failure semantics dependency, and request/response runtime-file lifecycle.

Effective-date reliability is now a Layer 1 prerequisite. Downloaded historical Teams VTTs may have file modification times equal to download/acquisition rather than meeting occurrence; the observed July 10/13 standups exposed the gap. Planned remediation: trustworthy occurrence candidate selection before ID/provider-request minting, explicit --meeting-date override, controlled date repair, and warnings for modification-time fallback.

Roadmap (post-layer1): make meeting-ingest init help consuming repos wire iQ Context. Today init only scaffolds meetings dirs/toml/cache-gitignore/ledger. Proposal: when init detects .iq-context/ in the target root, print a hint to add the meeting artifact globs to .iq-context/config.yaml (or run iq-context init, which auto-detects them), and point at the Post-Ingest Capture protocol. Keep coupling loose: iq-context owns detection/doctor; meeting-ingest init just nudges. Note: capture protocol already travels machine-wide via ~/.claude/skills/meeting-ingest and ~/.codex/skills/meeting-ingest (both updated 2026-07-18); the skill may need a fallback line for repos without iq-context initialized.

UAT feedback from first external consumer run (HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics, Codex agent, 2026-07-18): engine contract held perfectly (no partial side effects across 4 failed phase-2 attempts, safe retries, mtime warning surfaced, capture protocol ran), but the session extractor had to guess the response envelope schema and discover it one validation rejection at a time (owner_related_party, stakeholder_name, then stakeholder+stakeholder_name both), eventually grepping schema.py in the meeting-ingest source repo from the consumer project. Also observed: low-confidence mtime date was noted but baked into meeting ID mtg-20260718-aa2fd13f with no escalation step; two stale handoffs re-reported with no cleanup command; slug truncated mid-word at 80 chars. Prioritized remediation: (1) ship the exact response contract with the phase-1 request payload or skill doc - filled example or JSON schema, not just a contract version string; (2) aggregate all provider-response validation errors in one report instead of fail-fast, plus optional validate-response pre-flight command; (3) skill protocol step: on date_confidence low at phase 1, confirm occurrence date and re-run with --meeting-date before minting. Backlog evidence: stale-handoff cleanup command, word-boundary slug truncation.

Review methodology decision (2026-07-19): the former CodeRabbit review/autofix process is retired. At every review-before-commit checkpoint, implementation agents must generate a repository-aware, ready-to-paste prompt for the human to give Claude Code. Claude review is read-only and must not mutate git or .iq-context state. The global ~/.agents/skills/code-review skill was replaced with this workflow and the obsolete ~/.agents/skills/autofix skill was removed. Meeting Ingest reinforces the policy in AGENTS.md and docs/claude-code-review-prompt.md.

note: North Star Review Board founding review (2026-07-20): unanimous verdict that Meeting Ingest has a credible, useful engine but an unproven low-ceremony product experience. Proposed, pending owner approval: internal/private-alpha posture; one-host Just Works Ingest as the next milestone; freeze broader feature expansion; reconcile product truth; do not change the canonical roadmap silently. Human decisions remain on product definition, first host, 0.1 audience, artifact mutability, date policy, milestone order, and bounded cleanup carryover.

Reconvened North Star Board (2026-07-20): all five seats recommend replacing the narrow Just Works Ingest framing with proposed Just Works Continuity. Sustained HTV use is authoritative value evidence; the six copied local records are excluded. Approved runtime certainty, read-only HTV/Spelman corpus reckoning, fresh reference-host proof, and approval-gated historical continuity form the proposed gate. Release remains internal/private alpha. No roadmap change or corpus mutation is authorized pending owner decisions.

Owner approved the continuity-centered product definition, Just Works Continuity as the next milestone, Claude Code as reference host, and maintainer-only private alpha audience. Read-only HTV/Spelman reckoning authorized and completed. Corpus adoption remains separately approval-gated. Six redundant local records were removed as complete state. Immutable build/channel and editable-development policies remain open.

Read-only HTV/Spelman corpus reckoning completed without consumer mutation. HTV has 38 current and 81 legacy unique ledger source identities with no overlap; all 81 legacy sources retain matching processed copies. Legacy artifact/signal linkage remains insufficient for automatic adoption. Spelman has one source-backed legacy bundle requiring date review.

Owner approved the Approved Runtime policies: consumers pin one exact immutable reviewed build; stable may announce but never silently install updates; build replacement is explicit; approved Claude Code client work blocks editable builds by default with an explicit, visibly development-marked maintainer override.

body: Dogfood: first Claude Code ingest of 2026-07-20 from HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics processed Migration Planning Sync successfully via the session provider. Manual occurrence-date confirmation converted low-confidence mtime dating to explicit manual confidence; engine produced markdown, 7 signals, archive, completed reconcile, and iQ capture, leaving inbox clean. Friction observed: provider-request and validate/ingest source paths behaved inconsistently and required absolute paths; the dedicated extraction agent consumed 107.2k tokens for one meeting; initial low-confidence request was superseded rather than reused; session-inbox reported stale handoffs that required manual investigation and deletion, including two artifacts for an already-completed meeting and one superseded request. These are concrete Just Works Continuity/readiness and cleanup UX inputs.

Dogfood semantic review for HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics meeting mtg-20260720-cf7f049d, Migration Planning Sync (2026-07-20): summary accurate; decisions accurate; actions correctly attributed and framed; signals strong; owner would use without editing and found the distillation valuable despite a long discussion with interleaved thought streams. One identity defect: generated artifact changed Baba's raw VTT label from 'Opeyemi, Baba' to 'Opeyemi, Baba (Contractor)'. Baba is a client-side employee, not a contractor. Inspection shows this is not a colleague-vs-client engine classification: the current Attendee schema carries display_name, raw_labels, role_context, and confidence only, and the renderer prints provider output directly. The source VTT labels Ken and Jean Francois as '(Contractor)' but not Baba, so the session extraction provider invented/copied the suffix; the wrong label also propagated into two durable signal records. This suggests validation should enforce attendee raw_labels and signal evidence speakers against normalized transcript labels rather than trusting provider-synthesized identity text.

Independent semantic audit of HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics meeting mtg-20260720-cf7f049d found the artifact largely faithful but confirmed four additional framing defects beyond Baba's fabricated Contractor suffix. (1) The Decentrix timing dependency is ~10:06 PM/the last run of the night, not 10:06 AM; AM is repeated in TLDR, T5, R3, and Q5 and Q5 is internally inconsistent with its 9:58 PM alternative. (2) 'Jim' was merged with attendee Jean Francois Harden at high confidence, but the call repeatedly says they still need to ask/message Jim and Ken had not talked to him that morning, suggesting Jim may be a separate non-attendee notebook owner; identity should remain unresolved/low-confidence unless corroborated. (3) TLDR says session exhaustion was 'traced to' transient commit conflicts, overstating causality; the transcript and artifact body explicitly leave root cause unresolved and consider Fabric/session behavior. (4) A5 presents moving Hetal toward a for-loop pattern as an agreed action, but Jean Francois proposed it and Ken/Baba immediately called it untenable/not possible; the actual action was reducing cadence and moving validation toward UAT. Verified all four directly against the artifact and archived VTT. Overall semantic output remains strong, with deterministic facts/identity grounding and proposal-vs-agreement classification as clear validation opportunities.

Approved Runtime dogfood diagnosis for HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics (2026-07-20): two executable installations exist. Global /Users/kmgdev/.local/bin/meeting-ingest is a frozen uv-tool package whose tracked meeting_ingest Python files exactly match main commit 3bc917de8c6072239848ed190c4c45889d6cf227, but metadata exposes only version 0.1.0 and direct_url=file:///Users/kmgdev/dev_projects/meeting-ingest, with no immutable commit identity; post-main hooks can replace it. HTV-local .venv/bin/meeting-ingest is editable and imports /Users/kmgdev/dev_projects/meeting-ingest/src/meeting_ingest. The maintained Claude skill runs 'uv run meeting-ingest'; from HTV this resolves the local .venv executable and therefore live working-tree code, currently including uncommitted cli.py and playbook.py changes. Thus the 2026-07-20 Migration Planning Sync ingest was development/editable execution, not an approved frozen-build proof. Before release-quality dogfood, implement Track 1 build identity/readiness/pin enforcement, select an immutable packaged build, update the skill away from ambiguous uv-run resolution, then remove the editable HTV package after explicit approval. Exploratory dev dogfood may continue only if explicitly labeled development evidence with path, module, commit, and dirty state recorded.

Processed meeting: TRACE3 Daily Stand-Up – Post-MVP Status Sync (2026-07-20), HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics artifact _local/project-context/meetings/2026-07-20-trace3-daily-stand-up-post-mvp-status-sync-2026-07-20.md. Key outcomes: Microsoft-side agent validation blocker, CRM history duplication bug, and weekend Spark session issue dominate; Dilip pressed for consolidated validation status ahead of a proposed Wednesday meeting gating UAT next week. Decisions: none (proposals only, per grounding rules). Action items: Josh Docken compile agent validation results; Kushali send stats report; Jim Haley track duplication bug; Mark follow up flagged item. 7 signals written to _signals/mtg-20260720-42840eae.jsonl; reconcile completed; occurrence date manually confirmed 2026-07-20; development-override runtime.

Processed meeting: Budget Pipeline Reconciliation: Wide Orbit vs. Bronze/Silver/Gold Case Statement Fix — Call with G, Kushali (2026-07-22), HTV-IQ-DataAnalytics artifact _local/project-context/meetings/2026-07-22-budget-pipeline-reconciliation-wide-orbit-vs-bronze-silver-gold-case-statement-f.md. Key outcomes: Ken and Kushali reconciled budget-pipeline validation between Wide Orbit and bronze/silver/gold layers after a case-statement fix; confirmed the 21st/22nd pipeline runs completed plus a later full run; agreed on daily bronze-first mismatch triage. 2 decisions, 2 action items, 5 signals written to _signals/mtg-20260722-18cde3f6.jsonl; reconcile completed; occurrence date manually confirmed 2026-07-22; development-override runtime.
